title: Cross-channel creative fatigue: TikTok often fatigues first and predicts Facebook decline
insight: Running the same creative across multiple channels (Meta, TikTok, Google, Snap) enables cross-channel fatigue detection. TikTok's younger audience and faster content cycle typically causes creative wear-out before other platforms. A declining CTR and rising frequency on TikTok can predict that the same creative will underperform on Facebook 1-2 weeks later, because audiences overlap. By creating a 'fatigue map' that tracks time-lagged performance peaks across channels, you can preemptively rotate creatives before the performance drop hits your highest-spend channel.
raw_excerpt: If you run the same ad creative on different platforms, trends on one network can foreshadow performance on others. The younger audience and fast content cycle on TikTok may lead to faster creative wear-out. This early CTR drop on TikTok can indicate that the same creative might soon underperform on Facebook.
actionable_steps: Build a cross-channel fatigue map tracking time-lagged performance peaks/declines per creative
Use TikTok as an early warning system: declining CTR there often predicts Facebook decline 1-2 weeks later
When a creative hits ~2M impressions on one platform, monitor for fatigue signals across all platforms
Preemptively rotate creatives on all channels when fatigue appears on the fastest-burning channel

title: Onboarding drop-off rate by acquisition source is a leading indicator of creative fatigue
insight: When an ad creative fatigues, it doesn't just reduce clicks — it changes the quality of users who do click. Fatigued creatives attract users who click out of boredom or annoyance rather than genuine interest, leading to higher onboarding abandonment. Track onboarding completion rates broken down by campaign/creative ID. A drop from 50% to 30% onboarding completion from a specific creative — while fresh creatives maintain 50% — is a strong fatigue signal. Adding a 'Where did you hear about us?' question in onboarding provides a qualitative signal: if self-reported ad attribution drops (e.g., from 40% to 25% saying 'Instagram Ads'), the ads are becoming less memorable.
raw_excerpt: Users coming from its long-running YouTube ad used to have a 50% onboarding completion rate. Now, only 30% of users from the same ad complete onboarding. Users from a fresh TikTok ad are still at 50%. The drop is a strong indicator of creative fatigue.
actionable_steps: Track onboarding completion rate segmented by acquisition source (campaign, creative ID, network)
Set alerts for onboarding completion drops >10pp from a specific creative while others hold steady
Add a 'Where did you hear about us?' question in onboarding to monitor self-reported ad attribution trends
When onboarding quality drops for a specific creative, refresh it immediately — don't wait for CPI/CPA to rise

title: Build saturation curves to find the exact spend threshold where creative returns diminish
insight: Saturation curves plot conversions (or trials) against spend/impressions to find the inflection point where returns diminish. For a meditation app example: $1K-$5K daily spend yields ~100 extra trials per $1K. Beyond $5K, returns sharply decline — at $8K, only ~20 extra trials per $1K. At $10K, almost zero incremental trials. The curve flatline is the saturation point — pushing more budget past this wastes money. Build these curves per creative to understand each one's longevity and compare lifespans. Use the saturation point as a guideline for budget caps and creative refresh timing.
raw_excerpt: New video ad: increasing ad spend ($1k to $5k daily) significantly increases daily trial sign-ups (each $1k ≈ 100 extra trials). The trial increase slows significantly beyond $5k daily spend. At $8k, an extra $1k yields only ~20 trials. At $10k, almost no new trials are gained.
actionable_steps: Plot spend vs conversions for each creative to build saturation curves
Identify the inflection point where each $1K of additional spend yields sharply fewer conversions
Set budget caps at or below the saturation point — don't waste budget on flat returns
Compare saturation curves across creatives to understand which have longer lifespans

title: SKAN 4.0 anonymity tier drops signal ad fatigue even when spend is stable
insight: On iOS, a powerful fatigue detection signal is monitoring SKAN 4.0 anonymity tier changes while ad spend remains stable. If your anonymity tier is decreasing (meaning Apple provides less granular data) while spend hasn't changed, it means downloads are declining — Apple limits data granularity when fewer users convert. This is a deterministic signal that creative performance is degrading, independent of any modeled or probabilistic data. Pair this with monitoring total postback volume (including nulls) as a proxy for total attributed conversions.
raw_excerpt: If you are running on SKAN 4.0, a great way to detect ad fatigue is to monitor whether the anonymity tier is decreasing while the ad spend remains stable. This means that due to a decrease in downloads, Apple is starting to limit the data you get back because fewer users are converting.
actionable_steps: Monitor SKAN 4.0 anonymity tier alongside ad spend — a tier drop at stable spend signals fatigue
Track total postback volume (including nulls) as a proxy for total attributed conversions
Use anonymity tier monitoring as a deterministic fatigue signal independent of modeled data
When tier drops, rotate creatives before waiting for CPA/ROAS metrics to confirm the decline

title: Blended Day 0 ROAS and subscription rate per 1000 impressions detect aggregate fatigue
insight: Build blended metrics that combine app store analytics and ad data to detect aggregate ad fatigue. Key metrics: (1) Day 0 revenue from new users excluding subscription renewals — when this dips, ads are collectively losing effectiveness. (2) New subscriptions or trial starts divided by total paid ad impressions — a cross-channel efficiency metric that declines when creatives fatigue. (3) Blended Day 0 ROAS — if historical 20% drops to 10% with constant targeting, it's likely creative fatigue causing lower-intent installs. These blended proxies smooth out per-channel noise and reveal portfolio-level fatigue trends.
raw_excerpt: Take the total new subscriptions or trial starts in a period and divide by the total paid ad impressions served. If over time each 1,000 ad impressions yields fewer subscribers, despite similar targeting, it likely means the creatives are less compelling due to fatigue.
actionable_steps: Track Day 0 revenue from new users (excluding renewals) as a portfolio-level fatigue metric
Calculate subscriptions per 1000 ad impressions as a cross-channel creative efficiency metric
Monitor blended Day 0 ROAS weekly — a sustained decline at constant targeting suggests fatigue
Use RevenueCat or similar tools to pull app store + ad data into unified blended metrics
